cuellar-henry.text: As of 2026-07-04, FEC filings show Henry R. Cuellar's campaign has raised $1,620,505 and spent $904,427.

On H R 1 (22-May-2025), Henry R. Cuellar voted Nay. A Yea vote passed the 2025 budget reconciliation package extending the 2017 tax cuts, adding new tax breaks (e.g. on tips and overtime pay), cutting projected Medicaid and SNAP spending, boosting border and defense funding, and raising the debt limit.

On H R 29 (7-Jan-2025), Henry R. Cuellar voted Yea. A Yea vote required federal detention of unauthorized immigrants charged with theft, burglary, larceny, or assaulting a law enforcement officer, and let state attorneys general sue the federal government over certain immigration enforcement decisions.

esparza-juan.text: As of 2026-07-04, FEC filings show Juan Esparza's campaign has raised $32,404 and spent $32,404.
No U.S. House roll-call voting record was in our data set for Juan Esparza (non-incumbent candidates have no floor votes to record). Public candidate filings are listed at the source below.

base.summary: TX-28: 2 candidate(s) on file for U.S. House of Representatives, TX-28. Bullets below summarize each candidate's FEC-reported campaign finance and, for sitting U.S. House members, selected 2025-2026 roll-call votes on file. This is comparative, sourced information only -- it does not recommend a choice.
base.caveats: Only facts present in our sourced candidate data set are shown; missing fields mean no public data was on file at build time. Voting records only exist for sitting U.S. House incumbents; non-incumbent challengers have no federal roll-call history.

now.text: Henry R. Cuellar already voted Yea on H R 29 (7-Jan-2025). A Yea vote required federal detention of unauthorized immigrants charged with theft, burglary, larceny, or assaulting a law enforcement officer, and let state attorneys general sue the federal government over certain immigration enforcement decisions. That's already decided for the current term.

long_term.text: If Henry R. Cuellar's vote on H R 29 reflects how they would vote on similar bills in the future, that pattern would likely keep shaping this issue over the next decade. This is a projection, not a guarantee.
long_term.assumption: Assumes Henry R. Cuellar keeps voting the same way on similar bills in future terms.

cuellar-henry.text: On H R 6703 (17-Dec-2025), Henry R. Cuellar voted Nay. A Yea vote passed a Republican-backed alternative to extending the enhanced ACA marketplace subsidies, restructuring health-insurance premium subsidy rules for individual-market plans.
On H R 498 (18-Dec-2025), Henry R. Cuellar voted Yea. A Yea vote barred state Medicaid programs from using federal Medicaid funds to cover gender-transition procedures for minors.

cuellar-henry.text: On H R 26 (7-Feb-2025), Henry R. Cuellar voted Yea. A Yea vote barred the President from declaring or enforcing a moratorium on hydraulic fracturing (fracking) for oil and gas without prior congressional approval.

On H R 7744 (5-Mar-2026), Henry R. Cuellar voted Yea. A Yea vote passed the Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2026, funding DHS (including CBP and ICE border security and immigration enforcement operations) for the fiscal year.

now.text: Henry R. Cuellar already voted Yea on H R 26 (7-Feb-2025). A Yea vote barred the President from declaring or enforcing a moratorium on hydraulic fracturing (fracking) for oil and gas without prior congressional approval. That's already decided for the current term and relates to farming, ranching, water rights, or rural infrastructure.

Henry R. Cuellar already voted Yea on H R 7744 (5-Mar-2026). A Yea vote passed the Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2026, funding DHS (including CBP and ICE border security and immigration enforcement operations) for the fiscal year. That's already decided for the current term and relates to farming, ranching, water rights, or rural infrastructure.
